economic_notes: War Lake Convenience Store Ltd. operates a convenience store with Video Lottery Terminals onsite. War Lake Hydro Trust monitors financial contributions from Manitoba Hydro related to Keeyask Hydro Electric development on the Nelson River.
notes: Large surplus of $7,073,370 in 2022 vs $481,282 in 2021, driven partly by deferred revenue recognition timing. Net financial position is net debt of $578,265. Consolidated entities include War Lake Administration, War Lake Social Services, War Lake Hydro Trust Funds, and War Lake Convenience Store Ltd. Capital expenditures of $6,982,023 in 2022 vs $1,821,923 in 2021 resulted in large increase in tangible capital assets from $13,556,081 to $19,667,118. ISC funding nearly doubled year over year from $6,467,697 to $11,631,042. total_government_transfers includes ISC ($11,631,042) + CMHC ($85,754) + Solicitor General ($11,575) + Province of Manitoba ($171,576) = $11,899,947. osr includes Other revenue ($3,157,920) + deferred revenue net ($3,899,433 - $4,186,980) + interest income ($0) = $2,870,373.
